reasoning: Both models performed only the initial PKGBUILD gate audit and both concluded the source-fetch portion (global scope + pkgver()) is safe — which is correct: the PKGBUILD clones from the official upstream github.com/google/fonts via git+https, uses SKIP checksums (standard for VCS), and pkgver() only runs benign git rev-list/rev-parse commands. However, neither model reviewed prepare()/build()/package() functions or any .install hooks, because makepkg --nobuild failed to download the (very large) sources and the audit aborted. The package executes a real package() function and likely prepare() logic that was never analyzed. Therefore the overall package safety is inconclusive even though no malicious indicators were found in what was reviewed.
disagreement_analysis: No substantive disagreement. Both models reached identical conclusions (PKGBUILD source-fetch is safe) and both ended in the same SKIPPED state due to the makepkg --nobuild download failure.
coverage_issues: 0 files reviewed beyond the PKGBUILD gate; prepare(), build(), and package() functions never assessed
Source download failure prevented agent-stage file review entirely — the trigger reason (shallow review)
false_negatives: Unreviewed prepare()/package() logic could contain filesystem operations (rm/mv/install) with unexpected scope — not analyzed because sources failed to download
The package installs thousands of font files; any post-install hook behavior was not verified
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD prepare(), build(), and package() functions
Verify package() only installs font files under appropriate prefix and does not run network/exec payloads
learnings: makepkg --nobuild can fail for legitimate reasons (huge repos like google/fonts, timeouts, bandwidth) — the audit should fall back to static review of PKGBUILD build/package functions instead of skipping entirely when download fails
A download failure should not be conflated with a clean result; the tool should clearly mark such packages as 'inconclusive/manual-review' rather than implying no issues
For large VCS font packages, consider a shallow clone or skipping actual source download while still statically analyzing all PKGBUILD functions and .install hooks

The initial PKGBUILD gate prompt correctly scopes only to source-fetch safety; both models reasoned accurately within that scope — the gap is purely operational (download failure), not reasoning quality
